1.title: Shutter Speed
1.description: Shutter speed dictates how long the camera sensor is exposed to light. Fast speeds freeze action, while slow speeds blur motion.

2.title: Aperture
2.description: Aperture controls the size of the lens opening. A larger aperture (smaller f-number) captures a blurry background (shallow depth of field).

3.title: ISO
3.description: ISO measures the sensor's sensitivity to light. A higher ISO brightens the image in dark settings, but introduces visual noise or 'grain'.

1.question: Scenario 1: The Feline Portrait
1.description: Isolate the orange kitten against the wildflowers by completely blurring the background. Cats twitch unpredictably, so freeze the action entirely. Keep the sensor noise perfectly clean in this bright sunlight.

2.question: Scenario 2: The Studio Product
2.description: Isolate the Aura Botanica bottle from the distracting background using a shallow depth of field. It's a static studio shot, so a standard shutter speed won't blur it. Use moderate ISO to compensate for indoor lighting without ruining it with heavy grain.

3.question: Scenario 3: The Stadium Delivery
3.description: Capture the fast-paced bowler mid-delivery, but ensure the stadium crowd behind him is also in sharp focus. Freeze the action completely. You will need maximum sensor sensitivity to expose this correctly with such a deep focus.

4.question: Scenario 4: The Heroic Ledge
4.description: The superhero is perched completely still on the ledge. Isolate her from the distracting city skyline by melting the background into a creamy blur. Keep the daylight exposure clean, and use a standard shutter speed since she isn't moving.
